final_chunks.text:
WEDNESDAY WEEKLY REVERSAL
This is the bearish version of the Wednesday Weekly Reversal. An old high or retail resistance level is identified. High or medium impact news on Wednesday runs price above it. The market rejects or 'discounts' that news event and reverses, moving aggressively lower.
final_chunks.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 07 - Short Term Trading Market Maker Manipulation Templates_Handbook.blocks.json

final_chunks.text:
WEDNESDAY WEEKLY REVERSAL (BEARISH)
This market profile is bearish, indicating an expectation for lower prices. Drives higher into a higher time frame premium array to induce buy stops. When the market is trading at a long-term or intermediate-term high.
final_chunks.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 07 - Short Term Trading Defining Weekly Range Profiles_Handbook.blocks.json

final_chunks.text:
CONSOLIDATION MIDWEEK DECLINE
On Wednesday (or Tuesday), the market trades up into a premium fair value gap, liquidity void, or bearish A high or medium impact news event occurs on Wednesday (London or New York open) as a catalyst.

final_chunks.text:
WEDNESDAY HIGH OF THE WEEK
This profile is generally bearish, indicating an expectation for lower prices. Price hovers below a higher time frame premium array on Monday and Tuesday. Then rises into that higher time frame premium array on Wednesday to form the high of the week.
final_chunks.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 07 - Short Term Trading Defining Weekly Range Profiles_Handbook.blocks.json

final_chunks.text:
WEDNESDAY HIGH OF THE WEEK
For this scenario, the market is primarily bearish. The market trades up into Wednesday into a premium market, filling a fair value gap or liquidity void, or trading into a bearish order block (monthly, weekly, daily).
final_chunks.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 07 - Short Term Trading Market Maker Manipulation Templates_Handbook.blocks.json

final_chunks.text:
WEDNESDAY LOW OF THE WEEK
Another scenario for Wednesday Low of the Week, for a bullish market. The market trades down into a discount Market, specifically retesting an old monthly, weekly, or daily high This implies the market traded up, broke out, and is now coming back down.
final_chunks.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 07 - Short Term Trading Market Maker Manipulation Templates_Handbook.blocks.json

AM DECLINE PM REVERSAL
In the PM session, we anticipate a run on the daily low or the low seen in the lunch hour.

final_chunks.text:
AM DECLINE PM REVERSAL
The 'AM Decline PM Reversal' setup. In the morning, we'll be looking for the Index SMT Divergence on the highs, going from the London session into the 9:30 AM equities opening. One of those three indices will be the bearish divergence, indicating a New York AM session decline.
final_chunks.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 10 - Index Futures - Index Trade Setups_Handbook.blocks.json

final_chunks.text:
AM DECLINE PM REVERSAL
In this diagram, the AM session low is suggesting the low forms above a 15-minute or 60-minute PD array. Therefore, a lower low could be expected in the afternoon, trading down into these 15-minute or 60 minute PD array.

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE PM TREND
The true day high or low will tend to form between 3 PM and 4 PM New York time, which is typically the last Between 1 PM and 4 PM New York time, there is typically a trend or a price swing seen daily. This is referred to as the PM Trend or the afternoon swing.

INSTITUTIONAL SPONSORSHIP AND MARKET MAKER BUY PROFILES
Wednesday marks a break of a short-term high, revealing institutional sponsorship. Each bullish order block, characterized by a down candle or series of down candles, is extended in time, showing new long positions being capitalized.

NEW YORK PM SESSION
Trading extended into the New York PM session, starting at 1:30 PM. The same Fair Value Gap (FVG) was utilized, extended in time. This approach, which "cuts through candles," differs from traditional supply and demand.

SIGNALS OF INDECISION AND REVERSAL
Very rarely do you see both sides of the previous day's candle tagged or traded through. If it does, it's usually indecisiveness or it's going to be a reversal soon, and they're clearing the board on both sides of the marketplace.

ANOTHER OUTSIDE DAY EXAMPLE AND REVERSAL
Here's another scenario here, outside day. We have the candle here, the high comes in at 93. 23 and the low is 92. 81. The very next day the high is taken out 93. 24, the low is 92. 75. So reversal day.

final_chunks.text:
DEFINITION AND IMPORTANCE
A reversal is when price moves in the opposite direction of the current trend. When looking for reversals, we directly couple that with ICT tools of liquidity pools. When price reverses direction, it indicates that the market makers have run levels of stops.
final_chunks.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 1 - Elements Of A Trade Setup_Handbook.blocks.json
